issue_title: Index out of bounds while loading skirmish custom map
issue_body:
<!-- This is a guideline that shall help you to include information we need to understand and fix the issue you experienced. Please follow the instructions and replace any placeholders that are written in capital letters. Instructions like this comment will not be visible in your report. -->

<!-- Important: Help us to avoid duplicates and use the search function to find existing reports for your issue. Please do not submit duplicate reports! Try to help others to find your report by using a precise title. -->

## Issue Summary
<!-- Please provide a a clear and concise description of what the issue is below. -->
The skirmish game crashes on loading my own custom map with a index out of bounds exception.
(Cnc)

## Reproduction
<!-- Please provide information about how the issue can be reproduced below. -->
Tested with "release-20200503"

1. Select Skirmish mode
2. Select the map "Rumble in the jungle 2"
3. Choose one cabal bot player on 2nd slot
4. Select the start position "A" for the player
5. Select the start position "G" for the bot
6. Start the game -> the game crashes

## Expected behavior
<!-- Please explain what you expected to happen below. -->

The game should just start, or the map editor should tell me if something is wrong with the map.
I have chosen 8 Multiplayer positions in the map, I thought this should also work for the skirmish mode, am I wrong?


## Screenshots / Screen recordings / Replays
<!-- If applicable, attach screenshots, screen recordings or replays to help explain your problem. -->

[rumbleInJungle2.zip](https://github.com/OpenRA/OpenRA/files/4618608/rumbleInJungle2.zip)
[exception-2020-05-12T194406Z.log](https://github.com/OpenRA/OpenRA/files/4618610/exception-2020-05-12T194406Z.log)
pr_title: Disable non-neutral owners for player spawns in map editor
pr_body:
Fixes #18092 

In my opinion it makes sense to remove other actors from the owners dropdown in map editor since player spawns assigned to anything other than neutral will cause crashes when spawns are unassigned

I've also added a check if we are trying to get a spawn and raise an exception if we do, however this approach is far from ideal and will still crash the entire game. I would really appreciate it If anyone could give me a lead on how could we recover from this state.
